title: Maackia tenuifolia
intro: Maackia tenuifolia, also known as Tochu or Chinese Maackia, is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Japan. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Maackia tenuifolia include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, such as maackiain and 7,3'-dihydroxy-4'-methoxyisoflavone. Maackia tenuifolia is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, as well as being formulated into capsules, tablets, or tinctures for modern herbal use.
taxonomy: Maackia tenuifolia is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a legume native to China and other parts of East Asia. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Maackia, which is a small genus of deciduous trees and shrubs. Maackia tenuifolia is often used as a medicinal plant, and its seeds and leaves are used in traditional medicine.
